# truenas_vm (Data Source)

Provides information about a virtual machine on TrueNAS SCALE.

## Example Usage

```terraform
data "truenas_vm" "example" {
  id = 1
}

output "vm_state" {
  value = data.truenas_vm.example.status
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `id` (Number) The numeric VM ID to look up.

### Read-Only

- `autostart` (Boolean) Whether the VM starts on boot.
- `bootloader` (String) Bootloader type.
- `bootloader_ovmf` (String) OVMF firmware variant.
- `cores` (Number) Number of cores per socket.
- `cpu_mode` (String) CPU mode.
- `cpu_model` (String) Specific CPU model (when cpu_mode is CUSTOM).
- `description` (String) VM description.
- `enable_secure_boot` (Boolean) Whether secure boot is enabled.
- `memory` (Number) Memory in bytes.
- `min_memory` (Number) Minimum memory in bytes (for ballooning).
- `name` (String) The VM name.
- `shutdown_timeout` (Number) Shutdown timeout in seconds.
- `state` (String) Current runtime state (RUNNING, STOPPED, etc.).
- `threads` (Number) Number of threads per core.
- `time` (String) Clock configuration (LOCAL, UTC).
- `uuid` (String) VM UUID.
- `vcpus` (Number) Number of virtual CPU sockets.
